name: pool
description: A thread-safe, generic object pool. New(factory) builds a Pool[T]; Get() reuses a returned value (LIFO) or calls the factory when empty; Put(v) returns a value for reuse; Len() reports idle count. Concurrency safety is provided by a sync.Mutex guarding the backing slice. Standard library only, go 1.23, gofmt-clean, and verified build+vet+test+race green.

maintain.request: Add a WithMaxIdle functional option to New so callers can cap how many idle values the pool retains. Once the pool already holds that many idle values, Put must discard the returned value instead of growing the pool, bounding memory use. A cap of 0 (or negative) means unbounded, preserving current behaviour. Add tests covering the cap, the boundary at exactly the cap, the unbounded default, and that retained values are still reused.

review.bug: Put appends to the shared p.items slice without acquiring p.mu, so it races with concurrent Get and Put calls that read and mutate the same slice header and backing array.
keywords: put does not lock the mutex
